items.title: Book tells story of Timorese asylum seekers' daring escape to Darwin

children.content: Jose da Costa's escape from Indonesian-occupied East Timor in 1995 has been detailed in a new book launched at the Northern Territory Writers Festival this weekend.

gallery.altText: A Timorese man, smiling, black curly short hair, light olive polo shirt, coastal background.

thumbnail.altText: A Timorese man, smiling, black curly short hair, light olive polo shirt, coastal background.

items.title: Aboriginal families in Federal Court bid for traditional ownership recognition

children.content: Two groups of Indigenous families have claimed in the Federal Court they should be recognised as the traditional owners of the NT's Cobourg Peninsula.

image.altText: The coastline of a remote beach.

items.title: This herb could halt a major NT gas project

children.content: The NT government is arguing its planned mega Middle Arm gas industry precinct will be needed to help shore up Australia's future fuel and energy supplies — but it has just identified that a tiny endangered herb could be growing in the way.

image.altText: A small herb plant, green with three leaves growing out of a stone / pebble ground area.

items.title: Leaked data shows NT diphtheria cases surged a month before alert was issued

children.content: Internal Northern Territory Health data leaked to the ABC reveals diphtheria case numbers surged in the NT a month before a public health alert was issued. The data also offers the first public insight into the regions hit hardest by the outbreak.

gallery.altText: A nurse injecting a needle into the arm of a patient who is getting vaccinated.

thumbnail.altText: A nurse injecting a needle into the arm of a patient who is getting vaccinated.

items.title: Indonesian importer buys up big at NT's biggest bull sale

children.content: The Northern Territory's biggest bull sale has recorded one of its strongest results, despite high fuel prices and a big wet season.

image.altText: Two brahman bulls, white with a hump neck in view, only one of their heads visible, people in the background holding paper.

items.title: Refugee who tried to flee the Taliban 15 times finally safe to play cricket

children.content: After the Taliban banned women's sport in Afghanistan, most of the women's cricket team ended up relocating to Australia. Now, they're on tour in the Northern Territory — "a happy ending" after a traumatic journey.

image.altText: Three women in view, one in centre mid-speech, wearing a pink jersey, black hijab, all standing on a green sports field.

items.title: At 15 Mohamed Semra made headlines. At 27 he’s making history

children.content: At just 27, Mohamed Semra is not your average mayor. He overcame a childhood stutter and a viral moment in a Melbourne Apple Store to make political history – and he is just getting started.

gallery.altText: Mohamed Semra wears black mayoral robes and chain while seated at solid timber chambers desk, his hands clasped

thumbnail.altText: Mohamed Semra wears black mayoral robes and chain while seated at solid timber chambers desk, his hands clasped

items.title: How Tiananmen Square vigils in Hong Kong have become unlawful

children.content: In May, Hong Kong's High Court began hearing closing arguments in the case of two democracy activists charged with inciting subversion for organising a candlelight vigil to remember Tiananmen Square.

image.altText: A woman wearing a black t-shirt and glasses stands in a high-rise building holding a battery-powered candle looking solemn

items.title: As NZ cracks down on gang members, one pastor is trying to heal them

children.content: In a country where there now more gang members than police officers, a former gang member turned Christian pastor is trying to turn things around for others by addressing the root causes of gang life.

image.altText: A heavily tattooed man with a traditional Māori facial tattoos sits on a motorbike, wears a vest with "My Brothers Keeper" patch

items.title: Smoke engulfed their cities. Did it make their children sick?

children.content: Years after bushfires covered their cities in smoke, mothers are left wondering if their children's chronic illnesses began with what was in the air before they were born.

gallery.altText: Sharnie Moran and her 18-month-old daughter Charlotte look on as thick smoke rises from bushfires
A child wears a facemask and steers a boat on the water with the red-orange sky behind them.
A view of the Palisades fire from a plane

thumbnail.altText: Sharnie Moran and her 18-month-old daughter Charlotte look on as thick smoke rises from bushfires
A child wears a facemask and steers a boat on the water with the red-orange sky behind them.

items.title: Is the government really overhauling employment services?

children.content: The government isn't rebuilding the Commonwealth Employment Service, but will its proposed changes fix a broken system?

image.altText: Rishworth speaks at the NPC lectern

items.title: The 'bread and butter' of a local court: More than 20 names on the traffic list

children.content: From hoons to drink drivers to people driving without a licence, one magistrate is trying to make sure the people brought before her know how quickly things can turn deadly on the roads.

image.altText: court sketch showing man in blue polo in front of a magistrate

items.title: How clay-court chaos is a return to the norm at the French Open

children.content: All the key favourites are now out of the men's draw at the French Open, but then again, this tournament has always been just a little bit different.

articles.title: Last Australian knocked out of French Open as Gauff's title defence ended

children.content: Aryna Sabalenka ends Australia's campaign at the French Open with a straightforward win over Daria Kasatkina, while Coco Gauff joins the slew of big names knocked out early in Paris.

image.altText: Daria Kasatkina hugs Aryna Sabalenka at the net at the French Open.

gallery.altText: Jannik Sinner leans over and looks up

thumbnail.altText: Jannik Sinner leans over and looks up

items.title: After nearly 80 years, the actors in this film have finally been understood

children.content: The "huge swathes" of Pitjantjatjara lines in the 1949 film Bitter Springs are at last translated.
Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

image.altText: A film poster with the words Bitter Springs plastered onto it, depicting Aboriginal men with traditional weapons

items.title: AUKUS partners unveil plan to develop underwater drones

children.content: The US, Australia and United Kingdom have unveiled a new "signature" project to develop cutting edge weapons systems and sensors for underwater drones as they try to reinvigorate the second pillar of the AUKUS agreement.

image.altText: Three men in suits stand for a press conference in front of the US, Australian and British flags

items.title: As Jane Austen turns 250, four new productions take on Pride and Prejudice

children.content: In a modern-day cost-of-living crisis, the classic 1817 novel's tale of inheritance and wealth still rings true.

gallery.altText: A man and several women dance on an enormous cake as confetti rains down on them.
A woman on a chaise lounge looks up from a book with a shocked look on her face.
Production still from Pride and Prejudice, showing four women on stage bending forward, mouths open as if shouting.

items.title: After five days in a dark cave, a scientist emerged with life-changing insights

children.content: Spending big money to do nothing in the dark is on the rise. Scientist Kiana Aran chronicled what happened to her when she tried it.

image.altText: headshot of Kiana wearing a black eye mask, there is a small round, black gadget on her forehead.

items.title: Former teachers' pets, rejoice. Guy Montgomery's spelling quiz is back

children.content: Welcome back to your Sunday morning quiz with everyone's favourite word wonderboy.

image.altText: Guy Montgomery beams as he stands in front of the Guy Mont set in front of a podium bearing a cup, purse and bucket.

items.title: Bioremediation of Agent Orange chemicals made in Sydney for Vietnam War

children.content: More than 50 years after the Vietnam War, students are working to remove Agent Orange remnants from a waterway connected to Sydney Harbour, where the chemicals were manufactured.

image.altText: A black and white photo of trees which appear to be dead with rubbish nearby the water with factories seen in the distance.

items.title: Periods stopped Vanessa participating in sport and she's not alone

children.content: Vanessa was forced to regularly skip sport because of her period. New research shows that more than 60 per cent of disabled athletes have done the same.

image.altText: A female swimmer is smiling as she adjusts her goggles, water is splashing around her

items.title: Rescuers shocked as remaining four Laos villagers emerge safely from cave on their own

children.content: Laos officials believe two other men may still be missing in the cave, although rescue crews say they have explored most of the tunnels and have not found any survivors or bodies.

image.altText: Two men in heartfelt embrace covered in mud

gallery.altText: Two men embrace, the one whose face is visible crying, in a rainforest.

thumbnail.altText: Two men embrace, the one whose face is visible crying, in a rainforest.

avatar.alt: Tim Swanston wearing a white shirt stands in front of a camera.

items.title: PSG wins thrilling shootout for back-to-back Champions League crowns

children.content: Paris Saint-Germain beats Arsenal in a tense shootout in the Champions League final to be crowned European champions for the second straight year.

image.altText: Marquinhos lifts the Champions League trophy with Paris St Germain teammates.

items.title: Aboriginal ownership of national parks celebrated at cultural festival

children.content: The Yuin people on the NSW South Coast celebrate two decades of Aboriginal ownership of two national parks.

image.altText: a man leans down over smoke

items.title: Unlikely material behind Whitsundays artist's fashion collection

children.content: In the front yard of her home in the picturesque Whitsundays, Felicity Chapman strips the last pieces of flesh and sinew from a strip of barramundi skin to make wearable leather art.

gallery.altText: A woman holds out a colourful overskirt with feathers and fish scales.

thumbnail.altText: A woman holds out a colourful overskirt with feathers and fish scales.

avatar.alt: A lady looks at the camera, smiling.

items.title: A planning rule wouldn't bend so the lights at this Canberra oval had to

children.content: If the six light poles skirting Mauka Oval look like they're on a lean, it's because they are. The 47-metre-high poles slant inwards because otherwise they couldn't be approved under an unusual Canberra planning rule.

image.altText: Very large curved floodlights stand above a major sportsground.

items.title: Donald Trump just passed his latest medical check. Here's what's in the report

children.content: Donald Trump's medical report shows the US president underwent a CT scan, cancer screenings and other preventative assessments carried out by 22 specialists.

image.altText: close up of donald trump speaking during a cabinet meeting at the white house

items.title: US won't let China impose 'hegemony' in Asia, Hegseth says

children.content: The US Defense Secretary addressed Asia's most important defence summit, but did not mention Taiwan as questions remain over a stalled weapons package.

image.altText: A man with grey and white hair speaks standing in front of a blue digital backdrop

items.title: Why 'irresponsible' dumping of mercury caused evacuations in outback town

children.content: An outback town's hospital, service station and school were affected when students discovered vials of mercury at the tip. Experts have explained why specialists have spent days doing testing in Cunnamulla.

image.altText: two people in protective suits walk along a footpath holding a bucket and neck of an atmospheric tester

items.title: Young motorbike rider critical after crash at motocross event

children.content: A 14-year-old boy has suffered life-threatening injuries in a collision with another motorbike rider at a junior titles event in Victoria's west.

image.altText: A motorbike rider on a dirt track.

items.title: Ukraine war reaches 'a new phase', where Kyiv has the advantage

children.content: Ukraine has developed AI-enabled drones capable of making precision strikes from hundreds of kilometres away, and it means using Russia's vital logistics routes has become a dangerous "lottery".

image.altText: A large military vehicle with flames and smoke coming out of it.

items.title: Teen's extraordinary recovery after surviving 'most extensive' shark bite

children.content: Fourteen-year-old Sam Nai survived a devastating shark attack with a desperate swim for survival, followed by 20 surgeries to reconstruct his abdomen.

items.title: Angus Taylor draws 'battle lines' in key Liberal Party address

children.content: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor accuses Prime Minister Anthony Albanese of starting a war on aspiration with the recent federal budget.

image.altText: angus taylor stands in front of liberal party posters and delivers a speech

items.title: US judge orders removal of Trump name from Kennedy Center

children.content: US President Donald Trump says his administration will transfer control of the Kennedy Center to Congress, after a judge ordered the removal of his name from the Washington venue and blocked his plans to close it for renovations.

image.altText: The John F. Kennedy Center for Performing Arts is seen in Washington.

items.title: Lil' Kim brings nostalgia to Vivid Sydney as festival continues

children.content: Rap superstar Lil' Kim performed mostly truncated versions of her hits at her Vivid Sydney show, but she gave the diehard fans in the audience exactly what they came for.

articles.title: Vivid Sydney drone shows cancelled after technical failure

children.content: All remaining Vivid Sydney drone shows have been cancelled following a technical failure at Monday night's show, where almost 90 drones fell out of the sky.

image.altText: drones inthe sky during a vivid show in sydney over darlingharbour as some fall from the sky
